textContents:
Keddie Family Information

        To put some light on my forebears, we'll go back as far as we
have any information. Two of my great, great grandparents were
Frank and Agnes (Keer) Walkenshaw. We believe they lived in Fife
County, Scotland. Grandparents of Thomas Keddie, my grandfather

        My great grandfather was George Keddie, Born May 22,1798, Fife
County, Scotland and died 1857 at Falkirk, Scotland. He married
Agnes Walkenshaw, born Oct. 21, 1816 in Scotland, and died 1887
in Falkirk.

        My other great grandfather and great grandmother, parents of
my grandmother, Janet Keddie, were Henry and Agnes (Leech) Taylor.
The dates of their births are unknown. Agnes died in 1903.

                        ----------------

        My grandfather was Thomas Keddie, born June 21, 1844, near
Falkirk, Scotland, and died Feb. 17, 1927, Sylvia Kansas.

        My grandmother was Janet (Taylor) Keddie, born Aug. 9, 1844,
in Scotland, and died Feb. 11, 1922, Sylvia. Both are buried in
the Fairview Cemetery, north of Sylvia.

                        ----------------

        Thomas Keddie married Janet Taylor in 1868, and they came to
the United States in 1870, and first settled in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.
He was a cabinet maker, and it is said that he built the original
steps in the Pittsburgh City Hall.

        In about 1877 they moved to Kansas, near Sylvia, where they
settled on a timber claim, which was farm land given by the govern-
ment in return for planting so many acres of trees. He also pur-
chased railroad land. Land given to the railroads to be sold to
raise money to build railroads, in this case the Atchison, Topeka
and the Santa Fe.

        Children born to Thomas and Janet Keddie were: Agnes, Jan. 18,
1869, in Scotland; George, Mar.9, 1871 at Pittsburgh; Jessie, Sept.
15, 1877, Sylvia" Annie, Oct. 26,1880, sylvia; Henry, Mar.9, 1882
and Thomas (my father) Apr. 16, 1884, Sylvia.

        This is who each of those married and their children:
Agnes - John Withroder: Everett, Thomas, Edward, George, Eleta, Gladys.
George - Mary Hamilton: George and Wilbur.
Jessie - George Ankerholz: Edith, Guy and Ada.
Annie - Howard Dennis: Henry.
Henry - Janie Davis: Winona, Eldred and Warren.
Thomas - Luttie Rhiter: Howard and Dorothy

                        ----------------

        Grandfather Keddie had these brothers and sisters: Agnes (Sharp),
George, Annie (Nichelson), Thomas (our grandfather), Jessie (Todd),
Frank, Adam, George and Mary (Todd). Little is known what became of
them. I believe George came to the U.S, It is thought Adam died at
sea and was buried there.

        Grandmother Keddie's bothers and sisters: Alexander Taylor,
Janet (Keddie), George, William, Marion, Jean (Spence), Lizzie (Wilson).
One of the girls came to Canada.

Some thing About Howard's Forebears

        Rather than try to work out a family tree, 1'm going to make
an effort to give somewhat of a run-down of what I know about some
of my ancestors.
        We'll start with the Keddie side: My great grandfather was
George Keddie, born May 22, 1798, Fife Co. Scotland and died 1857
at Falkirk, Scotland.
        My great grandmother was Agnes Walkenshaw, born Oct. 21, 1816
in Scotland. She died 1887 in Falkirk, Scotland.
        My other great grandfather and grandmother, parents of my
grandmother were Henry Taylor and Agnes Leech. The dates of their
births is unknown, but in Scotland. Agnes died in 1903.

                    ------------------

        My grandfather was Thomas Keddie, born June 21, 1844 near
Falkirk, Scotland, and died Feb. 17, 1927, Sylvia, Kans.
        My grandmother was Janet Taylor Keddie, born Aug. 9, 1844,
in Scotland, and died Feb. 11, 1922, Sylvia. Both are buried in
the community cemetery near Sylvia.  Fairview cemetery

                    ------------------

Note: The grandparents, on his mother's side, of Thomas Keddie
were Frank Walkenshaw and Agnes Keer.

                     -----------------

        Thomas Keddie came to the United States in the late 1870s
and first settled in Pittsburg. He was a cabinet maker, and it is
said that he built the steps in the old Pittsburg City Hall.
Just when he and my grandmother were married is not known.
Sometime in the 1877 they moved to Kansas, near Sylvia, where my
grandfather took up a timber claim, which was farm land that to
government gave in return for planting so many acres of trees. He
also purchased what was known as railroad land. Land given to the
railroads to be sold to get money to build railroads, in this case
the Atchison, Topeka and the Santa Fe.

        To Thomas and Janet were born Agnes, Jan. 18,1869, in Scotland;
George, Mar. 9, 1871 at Pittsburgh; Jessie, Sept. 15, 1877, Sylvia;
Annie, Oct. 26, 1880, Sylvia; Henry, Mar.9, 1882, Sylvia; and
Thomas (my father) Apr. 16, 1884, Sylvia.

        The following is who each of those married, and their children:
Agnes - John Withroder: Everett, Thomas, Edward, George. Eleta, Gladys.
George- Mary Hamilton: George and Wilbur.
Jessie - George Ankerholz: Edith, Guy and Ada.
Annie - Howard Dennis: Henry.
Henry - Janie Davis; Winona, Eldred and Warren
Thomas - Luttie Rhiter; Howard and Dorothy.

                     |||||
                        --------------

        Grandfather Keddie had the following brothers and sisters:
Agnes (Sharp), George, Annie (Nichelson), Thomas (Our granfather),
Jessie (Todd), Frank, Adam, George and Mary (Todd). Little is know
what became of them. I believe George came to the U.S. It is thought
that Adam died at sea and was buried there.

        Grandmother Keddie's brothers and sisters: Alexander Taylor,
Janet (Keddie), George, William, Marion, Jean (Spence), Lizzie (Wilson).
To my knowledge only one left Scotland and came to Canada.

        Grandfather Keddie had the following brothers and sisters:
Agnes (Sharp), George, Annie (Nichelson), Thomas (Our grandfather),

        Grandmother Keddie's brothers and sisters: Alexander Taylor,

                               Compiled by Howard Keddie

Excerpt from book "The Surnames of Scotland- Their Origin,
      Meaning and History" by George Frasier Black. 1946

KEDDIE: Kedie, Keddy, Kiddie, Kiddy. The ship of John Kid, a
Scotsman, was wrecked at Holkham, Norfolk, England, in 1388.
John Kady in record at Dysart, 1577, Margaret Keddie in Falsyde,
ParishofRoberton, 1623, Dorathia Kadie, heir of Alexander tailor
in Edinburgh, 1616, and James Keddie, hat maker there in 1635.
David Kediein Weyms, 18630, James Keddie was bewitched there in
1639, and Robert Keddie was elder in Dysart in 1641. Alexander Ceddy
was fined for 'Straicks and ryot' in 1664 and Donald Keddie in Moy,
Caithness, to be apprehended as a rebel in 1670. From (Mac) Keddie.
The name John Kede was probably from another source.
MacKEDDIE, MacKiddie: Forms of MacAdie. Donald M'Keddie, piper
in King Edward, was accused in 1647 'for drinking the devil's
health and pyping to those who drank it.' The form Maceddie is
found in Inverness. McKeddey 1684 and MacKedy 1647.




Doug: The above was sent to my by a James Keddie, Jr., who at tht
time was working for a book shop in Boston. His father was born
in Walkerburn, on the river Tweed in the County of Peebleshire,
not far from Abbotsford. He questioned whether there was any ,
connection between our families.

Thomas and Janet Keddie in Scotland
Howard's grandparents. Person in back is
a relative. Thomas shipped the Ford to
                # 3
Scotland in 1910 and toured Scotland
